title: After prior uproar over removal, new mayor to restore Italian flag road lines to Newton
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Newton Mayor Marc Laredo announces restoration of Italian flag road lines on Adams Street.
Controversial removal last summer was due to federal safety requirements, previous mayor cited high crash rates.
New road layout with designated parking lanes allows for return of red, white, and green lines.

title: Student charged in Danvers murder allegedly told police he ‘wanted to kill someone for a long time’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion in the death of 68-year-old Janet Swallow in Danvers.
DeMayo allegedly confessed to the police, stating he planned to kill for a long time, with no known victim connection.
DeMayo to undergo psychiatric evaluation while in custody; the crime is considered a random act of violence.

title: Revolution announce return of original homegrown player Diego Fagundez on 1-year contract
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Diego Fagundez returns to the New England Revolution on a 1-year contract with an option for 2027.
Fagundez, the Revolution's original homegrown player, has recorded 79 goals and 79 assists over 424 MLS games.
The Revolution, under new head coach Marko Mitrović, lost their first two games in the 2026 MLS season.

title: Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will close from 9 p.m. Sunday to 5 a.m. Monday for tunnel maintenance.
Detours will redirect traffic from North Harvard Street to Memorial Drive via Eliot Bridge and Anderson Bridge.
The overnight closure follows the South Boston St. Patrick’s Day parade; the Red Line will offer rush hour service.

title: South Boston to crack down on public drinking at St. Pat’s parade, as BPD warns about spiking
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston officials vow tougher enforcement against public drinking at St. Patrick's Day parade following chaos last year.
Boston Police Department warns attendees about risks of drink spiking during upcoming St. Patrick's Day celebrations.
City officials collaborate with education leaders to notify students on public safety protocols and legal consequences.

title: For $575,000, a historic New Hampshire home with seven laying hens
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Historic 1812 New England home in Wolfeboro, NH, listed for $575,000, features 3 bedrooms and 3 bathrooms.
The 2,017 sq ft home includes a screened-in porch, historic bread oven, and non-operable fireplace.
Property includes 3.3 acres, a chicken coop with 7 hens, a detached garage, and optional rehoming of chickens.

title: A longtime Cape Cod restaurant has closed
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth closed after over 20 years, as announced by the Kounadis family on social media.
The Kounadis family also owns Yarmouth House, Scally's Irish Ale House, and Tap City Grille in Cape Cod.
Jacqueline’s on Twenty Eight will replace DiParma, offering Italian cuisine and seafood with an Old Hollywood theme by April.

title: 2 of the most beautiful colleges in the U.S. are in New England, according to Travel + Leisure
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Yale University in New Haven, Conn. ranks 15th on Travel + Leisure's list of most beautiful U.S. college campuses.
Wellesley College in Wellesley, Mass. ranks 19th for its scenic landscape and historic architecture.
Travel + Leisure highlights Yale's diverse architectural styles and Wellesley's meandering pathways and 19th-century buildings.

title: Rhode Island stroke survivor relearns to speak practicing his Dunkin’ order
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Rich McMahon of Cranston, RI used his daily Dunkin' coffee order as speech therapy after suffering two strokes.
Doctors initially underestimated the severity of McMahon's condition, later identifying a carotid artery dissection as the cause.
Dunkin' recognized McMahon's recovery journey with free coffee for a year and Red Sox home opener tickets.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander wins superstar battle vs. Jaylen Brown as Thunder beat Celtics: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led the Thunder to a 104-102 victory over the Celtics, scoring 35 points with nine assists.
Gilgeous-Alexander broke Wilt Chamberlain's record for scoring 20 points in 127 consecutive games with his performance.
Jaylen Brown scored 34 points for the Celtics, but faced tough defense from Lu Dort and committed six turnovers.
